mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp, or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE < 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side shocks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of cases following >0.5% INDPRO declines, or the signal shows no predictive edge during VIX > 20 periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate forces monetary tightening expectations that compress equity multiples, generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a hit rate above 55%, or the mechanism breaks down when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 4% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.01, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP signals an economic downturn that immediately reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts, or the signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling real personal consumption indicates inventory accumulation that precedes corporate earnings revisions, driving negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases > 0.2% while RSAFS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The divergence condition fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a hit rate above 52%, or the mechanism loses predictive power when inventory-to-sales ratios are below historical medians.
